question: Does the image depict a colorful collage bullet journal page featuring torn-edge paper, washi tape, stickers, paper clips, coffee stains, and slight shadows?
Are the decorative patterns (background textures, stains, tape designs) free of any random, illegible, or unwanted text (only specified text appears)?
Is the main title at the top spelled exactly as: "My journal isn't meant to be perfect, it's meant to make sense of life and finally let my feelings breathe"?
Is the main title rendered in bold handwriting at the very top of the page?
Is the middle paragraph spelled exactly as: "Today I decided to do less and think more: write down how I feel first, then handle the tasks, take care of myself before taking care of my to-do list"?
Is the middle paragraph rendered in neat black pen on lined paper in the middle of the page?
Is the sticker text on the right spelled exactly as: "A reminder to myself: Don't use busyness to hide your real fears, it's okay to pause and take a breath"?
Is the text starting with "A reminder to myself..." rendered in a printed font on a colorful sticker to the right?
Is the text on the lower left strip spelled exactly as: "Love looks not with the eyes but with the mind, patience and slow progress are also a form of devotion"?
Is the text starting with "Love looks not..." rendered in a typewriter font on a narrow paper strip in the lower left corner?
Is the formula card text spelled exactly as: "(a+b)²=a²+2ab+b², remember the formula but also the derivation, don't just memorize without understanding" (ensuring superscripts are correct)?
Is the formula text rendered in a clear monospace font on a card in the middle area?
Is the receipt text spelled exactly as: "Today's expenses: Breakfast $5 + Coffee $4 + Bus $3 = $12, tracking spending keeps the mind at peace, adjust tomorrow"?
Is the receipt text rendered in a neat printed font on a small receipt sticker?
Is the to-do list text spelled exactly as: "To-Do List: Do laundry, call home, clean off the desk, sleep before eleven, don't procrastinate until too late"?
Is the to-do list text rendered in rounded handwriting on a sticky note in the lower right corner?
Is the washi tape phrase "If you feel you're going slow, just lighten your steps a little, but don't stop and don't look back" clearly readable at least once?
Is the washi tape text rendered in a printed style on a strip at the very bottom of the page?
